tasks.title: Complete photo harvests — Apple Photos and Google Photos into Fuseki
tasks.description:
## What
Run photo harvests from Apple Photos and Google Photos into Fuseki. Pipeline is 257h stale with 4 gaps.

## Why
Photos collection is a core Gathering domain. 10+ days stale means new photos not in the knowledge graph.

## AC
- [ ] Apple Photos harvest runs successfully into Fuseki photo graph
- [ ] Google Photos harvest runs successfully into Fuseki photo graph
- [ ] 4 coverage gaps identified and filled
- [ ] Harvest staleness drops below 24h
- [ ] Photo collection page reflects updated counts

Blast radius WIP overlap — cross-card collision detection via domain + value stream scopes
tasks.description:
Extend blast radius system to detect overlap between active WIP cards.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On WIP entry, role sees other active WIP cards sharing the same chunk (domain)
- On WIP entry, role sees other active WIP cards sharing the same sequence (value stream)
- Output format: 'Silas has #1309 in ops domain' style warnings
- No new systems — queries existing board data (chunk/sequence labels)

**Blast radius:** messages/board-client/src/sdk.ts, messages/board-client/src/blast-radius.ts

tasks.title: Doc drift audit — identify stale docs, enforce freshness as part of close-out
tasks.description:
## What

The If-Touched doc scan in session close-out isn't working. Docs in data/about/ are stale — decisions made, domains renamed, value streams redefined, but the docs still reflect old state. The close-out checklist says "scan for staleness" but roles skip it or do it superficially.

## Problem

- PRODUCT_VISION.md, GLOSSARY.md, SITE_MAP.md, API.md — likely stale after weeks of taxonomy/nav/domain changes
- Close-out If-Touched section lists docs to check but there's no enforcement or freshness signal
- No way to see at a glance which docs are current vs stale
- Roles have no incentive to update docs they didn't create

## AC

- [ ] Audit all docs in data/about/ — mark each as current, stale, or obsolete with evidence
- [ ] Add last-verified date to doc frontmatter or a freshness manifest
- [ ] Borg churn lens (#1286) should flag docs whose domain has changed but the doc hasn't
- [ ] Propose enforcement mechanism — gate, nudge, or dashboard visibility

## Context

Jeff noticed docs are "somewhat to very stale." This is the doc-drift gate (#515) that was never enforced. DEC-086 (domain-first) makes this worse — the map changed but the docs didn't follow. Third Borg lens: doc freshness.

tasks.title: Consolidate chorus-index scripts — 6 indexers → 1 with subcommands
tasks.description:
Phase 3 of Chorus consolidation (#1292).

Six indexer scripts with identical patterns → one with subcommands.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single chorus-index.sh with subcommands: artifacts|sessions|slack|spine|stories|journal
- [ ] All 6 original scripts removed
- [ ] session-watcher daemon calls unified script
- [ ] Incremental watermarks preserved per source type
- [ ] Migration path documented (Kade review request)

tasks.description:
Fix app-state.sh deploy to always recompile TypeScript before Docker build.

## Acceptance Criteria

- No path allows stale dist/ to be baked into the container image

tasks.description:
Phase 4 of Chorus consolidation (#1292).

Merge defect-poller + ops-agent into single chorus-ops daemon. alert-notifier stays independent (Silas review: alarm ≠ monitored system).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single chorus-ops LaunchAgent replaces two plists
- [ ] Error detection and health check both preserved
- [ ] alert-notifier remains independent
- [ ] Single log file, single plist

tasks.description:

Three processes serving one menubar icon → fold into andon-light.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] andon-light handles its own enrichment cycle (no external andon-enrich.sh)
- [ ] Input monitoring folded in or justified as separate
- [ ] Two fewer LaunchAgent plists
- [ ] Menubar status still updates within 30s of state change

tasks.title: Verify #1315 fix — AC survives move
tasks.description: Test card: create with AC, move through columns, confirm description persists. Verifying Silas's #1315 fix from PM side.

tasks.title: board-ts move wipes card description — read-then-merge missing on move path
tasks.description:
board-ts move wipes card description — read-then-merge missing on move path.

Root cause: moveToBucket synced the done flag via POST /tasks/{id} with only { done: bool }. Vikunja zeros omitted fields, wiping title and description.

Fix: include title + description in the done-flag sync payload, using the task state already read before the move.

**Blast radius:** board-client/src/client.ts

## Acceptance Criteria
- board-ts move preserves the existing card description (and all other fields) after moving
- A card with description + AC can be moved between columns without data loss
- Regression: board-ts update --description still works correctly after the fix

team-scan.sh and handoff-check.sh both run at boot, both check role state. Fold into werk-init.sh directly.

tasks.description: IoC pattern: don't ask roles to query Chorus first, have the hook do it. On Grep/Glob intercept, hook runs chorus-query.sh with the search term, prepends relevant hits to the result. Role gets enriched context without changing behavior. The principle is satisfied by infrastructure, not discipline. Ref: DEC-074, fitness function data shows 260 flags with flat trend — nudging isn't working.

Instrument demo seed capture — card ideas that emerge from demo conversations
Enrich People with relationship depth — beyond Friend/Family/Colleague to meaningful connection context
tasks.description:
## What
Enrich People contacts with relationship depth beyond flat categories (Friend/Family/Colleague). Capture how Jeff knows someone, shared history, role in his life.

## Why
2,279 contacts with shallow labels. Chandra Bommas is a Colleague but also Jeffs favorite manager. The category doesnt carry that. As Apple Contacts (#1269) and other sources fill in, the relationship layer needs to express meaning, not just type.

## AC
- [ ] Relationship model extended beyond Friend/Family/Colleague (e.g. mentor, collaborator, neighbor, family-extended)
- [ ] Ontology property for relationship context/notes (jb:relationshipNote or similar)
- [ ] People page shows relationship context when available
- [ ] Stories that mention people link to their People entry
- [ ] Design doc: what relationship depth means for Gathering (not just more fields)

tasks.title: Harvest Apple Contacts into People — import contacts from macOS Contacts.app into Fuseki people graph
tasks.description:
## What
Harvest contacts from macOS Contacts.app (Apple Contacts) into the Fuseki people graph. Similar pattern to music and photo harvests.

## Why
People page has 2,256 contacts from Facebook/LinkedIn but none from Apple Contacts — the people Jeff actually communicates with daily. Family, local contacts, service providers. This is the relationship layer social networks miss.

## Dependencies
- Feeds into #1196 (People + Network merge) — more data makes the merged page richer
- Follows same harvest pattern as music and photos
- May need TCC permission for Contacts access (similar to #1246 pattern)

## AC
- [ ] Apple Contacts exported (vCard or Contacts framework)
- [ ] Contacts mapped to jb:Person RDF triples (name, email, phone, org, notes)
- [ ] Deduplication against existing Facebook/LinkedIn contacts
- [ ] Ingested into Fuseki people graph
- [ ] People page shows Apple Contacts alongside existing sources
- [ ] Source attribution: each contact tagged with origin (apple/facebook/linkedin)

tasks.description:
Three triage categories from #534:
- Books to shelve
- Books that might shelve if space allows (most likely not)
- Books to keep but not shelve

Work one box at a time from the library. No deadline. Photos/OCR feed the books domain in Gathering (141+ and growing).

tasks.title: Borg assessment dashboard — live model-vs-reality view under Product Vision
tasks.description:
## What

Live dashboard page showing the taxonomy map assessed against the current codebase. For each domain × value stream stage, Borg classifies:

- **Build** — domain defined in taxonomy but no page/handler exists yet
- **Refactor** — page exists but in the wrong domain, wrong nav position, or missing boundaries
- **Rewrite** — page exists but was built ahead of the map and can't be salvaged by moving

Color-coded grid: green (conforms), yellow (refactor), red (rewrite), grey (build needed).

## Why

DEC-086 (domain-first development) establishes the taxonomy as the governing artifact. This dashboard is the enforcement surface — makes model-vs-reality visible at a glance. Chorus designs the model, Borg assesses conformance.

## AC

- [ ] Page at a Product Vision nav location (exact placement TBD with v5 nav)
- [ ] Domain × value stream grid with color-coded status
- [ ] Each cell links to the relevant page or card
- [ ] Assessment logic reads from taxonomy + live routes/handlers
- [ ] Three modes labeled: build, refactor, rewrite

tasks.title: Read Wardley on conversational programming lineage — Chorus positioning reference
Help Jeff simplify personal password management tooling
Voice Analytics as Reflecting hub — aggregate posture, sentiment, session tempo, personal analytics
Borg two-phase delivery — hand drawings to HTML iteration, then build from validated prototype
tasks.description:
## What
Document and operationalize Borg's two-phase delivery pattern as a repeatable methodology.

## Phase 1: Iterate with client (spike/discovery)
- Hand drawings → HTML prototypes, rapidly iterated
- Refine UI + OWL + value stream + domains as we go
- 2-3 cycles
- Identify integrations, security, and harvest considerations along the way
- Output: validated prototype that captures intent

## Phase 2: Build from 1 (commitment)
- The iteration output becomes the spec — estimable, buildable
- Code + tests + UX + tickets + logs + monitoring + security + documentation
- Full blast radius coverage (DEC-072)

## Why
This is Jeff's natural working pattern — validated by the Athena experience at Staples. Phase 1 is discovery through making. Phase 2 is commitment informed by understanding. Maps to spike/discovery/commitment taxonomy.

## Athena lesson
Process weight kills adoption. Phase 1 must stay lightweight — sketches, conversation, rapid cycles. Phase 2 inherits rigor from the system, not from human paperwork.

## AC
- [ ] Pattern documented in About library
- [ ] Card lifecycle integrates the two phases (spike → discovery → commitment maps to Phase 1 → Phase 2)
- [ ] Borg can execute Phase 1 autonomously given hand drawings as input

## Tags
[medium] [discovery] chorus methodology

Fix Kade demo pattern — align with team demo tuning (DEC-048)
tasks.description:
## What
Two issues with Kade's demo pattern:
1. **No invitation** — Kade demos directly to Jeff without notifying Wren or Silas. Breaks DEC-048 proving gate (Wren accepts, not the builder). Wren only learns about acceptance when Jeff relays it.
2. **Style drift** — demos are verbose/procedural instead of visual-first (show it, wait for Jeff, Wren accepts).

## AC
- Kade's /demo skill sends a nudge to Wren (and Silas if infra-touching) BEFORE presenting to Jeff
- Demo output matches team pattern: frame → /look evidence → wait for Jeff → Wren accepts
- No verbose narration — visual evidence first, words second
- Verify nudge delivery so Wren can observe the demo live

## Context
Jeff observed both gaps during #1197 acceptance. Compare to Silas #537 demo (nudged Wren, followed tuned pattern). The demo skill fragment in Kade's CLAUDE.md needs to enforce the routing, not just the format.

Fix photo import for book catalog — errors on batch import, improve UX for 12+ photos
tasks.description:
## What
Jeff photographed a box of books (50 photos). Import flow had issues:
- 50 photos at once didn't work well (too many? error? timeout?)
- Scaled down to 12 — worked but had errors during import
- Was able to tag cover + publisher after import
- Relates to #534 (physical catalog capture)

## AC
- Batch import of 50+ photos completes without errors
- Progress indicator during import (not a silent spinner)
- Clear error messages when individual photos fail (don't abort the whole batch)
- Import UX handles Jeff's actual workflow: phone photos → bulk upload → tag
